frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Bro I don’t mind makeup, but some girls overdo it so much that it feels fake yaar. Like either be natural or at least don’t make it look like a different person offline, but then if someone puts zero effort also it looks like she’s not taking care of herself. Balance hona chahiye.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it places women in a double bind: criticizing visible makeup as fake or deceptive while also expecting them to maintain appearance and not 'put zero effort.' It encodes the makeup paradox through natural-vs-artificial policing and appearance obligation.
original_frame: The Character Double Standard: Women with Male Connections are Loose, Men with Female Connections are Desirable
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 27. Single. A man in Bengaluru. You are on a third date with a woman named Kavya who mentioned casually that most of her close friends are male Ñ she finds it easier, less drama, she has always been like this. You are back home texting your best friend about the date. You are telling him what Kavya said about her friend group and what you think it means and whether you see this as a red flag and what kind of woman has mostly male friends and whether you would pursue something serious with her.
